name: Dundee Cake
recipeInstructions:
(For an 18cm cake tin)
1. Grease and line the base and sides of the tin. Grease the paper lining.
2. Mix the sultanas, currants, raisins, peel, cherries, and ground almonds together.
3. Beat the butter until soft and creamy. Add the caster sugar and lemon rind, beating until light and fluffy in color and texture.
4. Beat the eggs and add them gradually to the creamed mixture, beating well after each addition.
5. Sift the flour and baking powder into the creamed mixture with the sherry and all the dried fruit. Mix lightly.
6. Pour the batter into the prepared tin, spreading it evenly and slightly hollowing out the center. Cover the surface with almonds.
7. Bake at 160°C (325°F) for 2.5 - 3 hours or until a skewer inserted into the cake comes out clean.
8. Cool the cake in the tin for 15 minutes, then turn it onto a wire rack to cool completely.
